This took us deeper than expected,

- A quick note about what I'm trying to do here.
I am not the person to give you

Clarity on the story of fascism,

- Before the First World War,

Italy was actually on the winning side,

Italy felt cheated.

It was, in their words, a "mutilated victory," a bad deal.

So Italy and Germany,

Before World War I,
Mussolini was a rising star in Italian socialism.
He ran an Italian socialist paper.
He advocated for workers' rights.
But once World War I started,
Mussolini breaks with the socialists

So they kick him out of the party.
And this is where he pivots hard.
He starts his own pro-war nationalist newspaper,
all about pride and sacrifice

Italy does enter World War I,

He gets wounded in a training exercise,

even more resolved in his vision of a strong Italy.
But now with a new set of beliefs

And he starts to share his beliefs.

He starts a club with a small group of other guys,
a lot of them ex-soldiers like him,
men who also feel humiliated

And he tells them that Italy's problems
are not only because of the war

that "mutilated victory,"

And even democracy itself, or liberalism,

Italy's democracy had, in his words,

They couldn't get anything meaningfully done

all these different viewpoints.
He says, Guys, this is Italy.
We're the historic home of the Roman Empire,

But Mussolini says that he has a solution.
He says that Italy must be ruled

Men who had been purified by violence and sacrifice

In his eyes, these were a new kind of man,
a new kind of elite,

And now these veterans had an obligation

and to replace it with a, quote, "trenchocracy,"
the aristocracy of the trenches.

So this is like our origin story here.

Not even a year after World War I has ended.

fascism begins as a nationalist movement
